concepts.label: Philosopher
concepts.definition: Person who studies fundamental questions about existence, knowledge, and ethics

concepts.label: Ad Hominem
concepts.definition: Attacking the person making the argument rather than the argument itself

concepts.label: Straw Man
concepts.definition: Misrepresenting someone's argument to make it easier to attack

concepts.label: Appeal to Authority
concepts.definition: Using an authority figure's opinion as evidence without proper justification

concepts.label: Appeal to Emotion
concepts.definition: Using emotional manipulation instead of valid reasoning

concepts.label: Appeal to Nature
concepts.definition: Arguing something is good because it is natural

concepts.label: Appeal to Tradition
concepts.definition: Arguing something is right because it has always been done that way

concepts.label: Appeal to Popularity
concepts.definition: Arguing something is true because many people believe it (bandwagon)

concepts.label: Appeal to Ignorance
concepts.definition: Claiming something is true because it hasn't been proven false

concepts.label: Red Herring
concepts.definition: Introducing an irrelevant topic to divert attention from the original issue

concepts.label: False Dilemma
concepts.definition: Presenting only two options when more exist

concepts.label: Slippery Slope
concepts.definition: Arguing that one event will lead to a chain of negative events without justification

concepts.label: Circular Reasoning
concepts.definition: Using the conclusion as a premise in the argument (begging the question)

concepts.label: Hasty Generalization
concepts.definition: Drawing broad conclusions from insufficient evidence

concepts.label: False Cause
concepts.definition: Assuming that because two events are correlated, one caused the other

concepts.label: Post Hoc Ergo Propter Hoc
concepts.definition: Assuming that because B followed A, A caused B

concepts.label: Tu Quoque
concepts.definition: Deflecting criticism by pointing to the accuser's similar behavior

concepts.label: No True Scotsman
concepts.definition: Redefining criteria to exclude counterexamples

concepts.label: Equivocation
concepts.definition: Using a word with multiple meanings in different parts of an argument

concepts.label: Composition Fallacy
concepts.definition: Assuming what is true of parts is true of the whole

concepts.label: Division Fallacy
concepts.definition: Assuming what is true of the whole is true of its parts

concepts.label: Gambler's Fallacy
concepts.definition: Believing past random events affect future probabilities

concepts.label: Burden of Proof
concepts.definition: Claiming something is true and demanding others disprove it

concepts.label: Moving the Goalposts
concepts.definition: Changing the criteria for proof after evidence is presented

concepts.label: Texas Sharpshooter
concepts.definition: Selecting data that fits a pattern while ignoring data that doesn't

concepts.label: Genetic Fallacy
concepts.definition: Judging something as good or bad based on its origin

concepts.label: Middle Ground Fallacy
concepts.definition: Assuming the truth is always a compromise between two positions

concepts.label: Loaded Question
concepts.definition: Asking a question that contains a presupposition

concepts.label: Affirming the Consequent
concepts.definition: Formal fallacy: If P then Q; Q; therefore P

concepts.label: Denying the Antecedent
concepts.definition: Formal fallacy: If P then Q; not P; therefore not Q

concepts.label: Non Sequitur
concepts.definition: Conclusion that does not logically follow from the premises

concepts.label: Cherry Picking
concepts.definition: Selecting only evidence that supports a position while ignoring contrary evidence

concepts.label: Sunk Cost Fallacy
concepts.definition: Continuing an endeavor because of previously invested resources

concepts.label: Naturalistic Fallacy
concepts.definition: Deriving ought from is, confusing natural properties with moral ones

concepts.label: Moralistic Fallacy
concepts.definition: Deriving is from ought, assuming the world is as it should be

concepts.label: Nirvana Fallacy
concepts.definition: Rejecting a solution because it is not perfect

concepts.label: Logical Fallacy
concepts.definition: Error in reasoning that renders an argument invalid

concepts.label: Logic
concepts.definition: Study of valid reasoning and argumentation

concepts.label: Confirmation Bias
concepts.definition: Tendency to search for information that confirms existing beliefs

concepts.label: Anchoring Bias
concepts.definition: Over-reliance on the first piece of information encountered

concepts.label: Availability Heuristic
concepts.definition: Judging likelihood based on how easily examples come to mind

concepts.label: Dunning-Kruger Effect
concepts.definition: Low-ability individuals overestimate their competence

concepts.label: Bandwagon Effect
concepts.definition: Tendency to adopt beliefs because many others hold them

concepts.label: Hindsight Bias
concepts.definition: Tendency to see past events as having been predictable

concepts.label: Framing Effect
concepts.definition: Drawing different conclusions from the same information depending on presentation

concepts.label: Status Quo Bias
concepts.definition: Preference for the current state of affairs

concepts.label: Loss Aversion
concepts.definition: Tendency to prefer avoiding losses over acquiring equivalent gains

concepts.label: Negativity Bias
concepts.definition: Greater sensitivity to negative than positive experiences

concepts.label: Optimism Bias
concepts.definition: Tendency to overestimate likelihood of positive outcomes

concepts.label: Self-Serving Bias
concepts.definition: Attributing successes to oneself and failures to external factors

concepts.label: Fundamental Attribution Error
concepts.definition: Overemphasizing personality and underemphasizing situational factors in others' behavior

concepts.label: Halo Effect
concepts.definition: Overall impression of a person influences specific judgments about them

concepts.label: Horn Effect
concepts.definition: Negative overall impression influences specific judgments negatively

concepts.label: In-Group Bias
concepts.definition: Favoring members of one's own group over outsiders

concepts.label: Out-Group Homogeneity Bias
concepts.definition: Perceiving members of other groups as more similar than they are

concepts.label: Mere Exposure Effect
concepts.definition: Developing preference for things through repeated exposure

concepts.label: Recency Bias
concepts.definition: Giving more weight to recent events than earlier ones

concepts.label: Primacy Effect
concepts.definition: Giving more weight to initial information

concepts.label: Peak-End Rule
concepts.definition: Judging experiences based on their peak and end moments

concepts.label: Sunk Cost Bias
concepts.definition: Continuing investment due to past costs rather than future value

concepts.label: Endowment Effect
concepts.definition: Overvaluing things simply because you own them

concepts.label: Choice-Supportive Bias
concepts.definition: Retroactively attributing positive features to chosen options

concepts.label: Survivorship Bias
concepts.definition: Focusing on successes while ignoring failures

concepts.label: Observer-Expectancy Effect
concepts.definition: Researcher's expectations influencing experimental outcomes

concepts.label: Placebo Effect
concepts.definition: Improvement caused by belief in treatment rather than the treatment itself

concepts.label: Spotlight Effect
concepts.definition: Overestimating how much others notice your appearance and behavior

concepts.label: Illusion of Control
concepts.definition: Overestimating one's influence over external events

concepts.label: Planning Fallacy
concepts.definition: Underestimating time and resources needed to complete a task

concepts.label: Curse of Knowledge
concepts.definition: Difficulty imagining what it is like to not know something you know

concepts.label: Projection Bias
concepts.definition: Assuming others share your beliefs and values

concepts.label: Attribution Bias
concepts.definition: Systematic errors in attributing causes to behavior

concepts.label: Authority Bias
concepts.definition: Tendency to attribute greater accuracy to authority figures

concepts.label: Automation Bias
concepts.definition: Over-reliance on automated systems

concepts.label: Belief Bias
concepts.definition: Judging argument strength based on believability of conclusion

concepts.label: Clustering Illusion
concepts.definition: Seeing patterns in random data

concepts.label: Conjunction Fallacy
concepts.definition: Assuming specific conditions are more probable than general ones

concepts.label: Decoy Effect
concepts.definition: Preference change when a third option is introduced

concepts.label: Default Effect
concepts.definition: Tendency to accept pre-set options

concepts.label: Denomination Effect
concepts.definition: Less likely to spend large denominations than equivalent small ones

concepts.label: Distinction Bias
concepts.definition: Overvaluing differences when comparing options simultaneously

concepts.label: Empathy Gap
concepts.definition: Underestimating influence of emotional states on decisions

concepts.label: Focusing Effect
concepts.definition: Overweighting one aspect of an event

concepts.label: IKEA Effect
concepts.definition: Overvaluing things you helped create

concepts.label: Illusion of Transparency
concepts.definition: Overestimating how well others understand your mental state

concepts.label: Impact Bias
concepts.definition: Overestimating the duration and intensity of future emotional states

concepts.label: Information Bias
concepts.definition: Seeking information even when it doesn't affect action

concepts.label: Just-World Hypothesis
concepts.definition: Belief that people get what they deserve

concepts.label: Moral Licensing
concepts.definition: Past good behavior allows future bad behavior

concepts.label: Normalcy Bias
concepts.definition: Underestimating the possibility of disaster

concepts.label: Ostrich Effect
concepts.definition: Avoiding negative information by burying one's head

concepts.label: Reactance
concepts.definition: Doing the opposite when feeling freedom is threatened

concepts.label: Representativeness Heuristic
concepts.definition: Judging probability by similarity to a stereotype

concepts.label: Rhyme-as-Reason Effect
concepts.definition: Perceiving rhyming statements as more truthful

concepts.label: Rosy Retrospection
concepts.definition: Remembering past events more positively than they were

concepts.label: Semmelweis Reflex
concepts.definition: Rejecting new evidence that contradicts established norms

concepts.label: Social Desirability Bias
concepts.definition: Presenting oneself in a favorable light in surveys

concepts.label: System Justification
concepts.definition: Defending and supporting existing social systems

concepts.label: Zero-Risk Bias
concepts.definition: Preferring to eliminate small risks entirely rather than reducing larger risks

concepts.label: Affect Heuristic
concepts.definition: Making judgments based on current emotions

concepts.label: Ambiguity Effect
concepts.definition: Avoiding options with unknown probabilities

concepts.label: Cognitive Bias
concepts.definition: Systematic pattern of deviation from rational judgment

concepts.label: Psychology
concepts.definition: Scientific study of mind and behavior

concepts.label: Mental Disorder
concepts.definition: Behavioral or mental pattern causing significant distress or impairment

concepts.label: Major Depressive Disorder
concepts.definition: Persistent feelings of sadness, hopelessness, and loss of interest

concepts.label: Bipolar I Disorder
concepts.definition: Manic episodes with or without depressive episodes

concepts.label: Bipolar II Disorder
concepts.definition: Hypomanic and major depressive episodes without full mania

concepts.label: Cyclothymic Disorder
concepts.definition: Chronic fluctuating mood with periods of hypomania and depression

concepts.label: Persistent Depressive Disorder
concepts.definition: Chronic depressed mood lasting at least two years (dysthymia)

concepts.label: Seasonal Affective Disorder
concepts.definition: Depression related to seasonal changes, typically winter

concepts.label: Premenstrual Dysphoric Disorder
concepts.definition: Severe mood symptoms in the luteal phase of menstruation

concepts.label: Disruptive Mood Dysregulation Disorder
